faqs.q: Does a relapse mean treatment failed?
faqs.a: No. A relapse isn't a failure and doesn't mean a person didn't try hard enough, since many conditions run in episodes where symptoms can return even when someone is doing everything right.

faqs.q: What are the warning signs of a relapse?
faqs.a: Many people learn their own early warning signs, which can include changes in sleep, pulling back from people, losing interest in usual activities, or the return of a familiar thought pattern. Spotting these early gives you and your clinician a chance to adjust care before symptoms take hold.

faqs.q: What raises the risk of relapse?
faqs.a: Stopping medication suddenly is a common trigger, which is why prescribers usually taper rather than cut off treatment. Major stress, poor sleep, substance use, and dropping helpful routines can also play a part.
